items.briefing:
Australian authorities are warning about a rise in "device code phishing" — a scam where criminals trick you into entering a code that hands over access to your accounts. The Australian Signals Directorate has flagged this technique as increasingly common, with criminal toolkits making it easier for attackers to run these scams at scale. Separately, a new survey highlights that Australian shoppers are concerned about security when buying through social media platforms like Instagram and TikTok Shop.

Apple also reported blocking over $2.2 billion USD in App Store fraud last year, a reminder that app-based scams remain widespread. **Practical tip:** Be cautious of any unexpected prompts asking you to enter a device code to "verify" or "sign in" — if in doubt, don't enter it and contact your IT team.

featured.title: ASD warns of device code phishing as Proofpoint tracks growing criminal toolkits
featured.quote: ASD warns Microsoft 365 users of device code phishing attacks from criminal toolkits.
